top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 82, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
When someone attacks on flat fenland roads with persistent wind exposure, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Tour of Cambridgeshire's flat fenland roads with persistent wind exposure rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour of Cambridgeshire's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour of Cambridgeshire. Train it until it feels boring.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 82 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Tour of Cambridgeshire's flat fenland roads with persistent wind exposure forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 82 miles.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Tour of Cambridgeshire's flat fenland roads with persistent wind exposure actually does to you.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 82 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
2-minute all-out efforts on flat fenland roads with persistent wind exposure. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
The aerobic base that lets you ride flat fenland roads with persistent wind exposure at tempo for 82 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Tempo is Tour of Cambridgeshire's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 82 miles on flat fenland roads with persistent wind exposure. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour of Cambridgeshire’s 82 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 82 miles of flat fenland roads with persistent wind exposure in East of England Showground, Peterborough, Cambridgeshire, England.
